title: quamoclit coccinea var. hederifolia
intro: Quamoclit coccinea var. hederifolia, also known as ivy climber or ivy-leaved clematis, is a flowering plant that is a variety of the clematis family. It is an annual vine with a rich history of medicinal use, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Quamoclit coccinea var. hederifolia can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
